summary: The U.S. Department of Transportation Office of Aviation Analysis (OAA) is seeking a contractor to provide an Airline Analysis and Scheduling Solution via a web-based application that delivers global airline schedule, airfare, and market demand data to support the agency's statutory responsibilities in analyzing domestic and international aviation policies, reviewing airline economic transactions, and supporting route selection proceedings. The solution must provide global origin-and-destination data, fare and service class information, fair share/QSI data, historical and current fleet information with cabin configurations, advanced booking data derived from airline industry sources such as GDS providers, and schedule data with flight-level granularity including flight numbers, aircraft types, and seat counts on a market-by-market basis, with historical data going back to at least 2005. The platform must support a minimum of 10 concurrent users with dedicated credentials, offer 24/7 access, provide bulk data export capabilities, weekly schedule updates, monthly demand and fare file updates, and include customer support services along with up to three days of annual user training at DOT offices in Washington, DC or virtually. This is a recompete of an existing contract held by Sabre, structured as a Firm Fixed Price award under full and open competition with a best value evaluation methodology, consisting of a one-year base period and four one-year option periods spanning approximately May 2026 through April 2031. The procurement is critical to OAA's mission-essential functions including evaluating Antitrust Immunity applications between U.S. and foreign carriers under 49 U.S.C. 41309, supporting DOJ-led merger analyses, conducting carrier selection decisions for slot-constrained airports, and providing ad-hoc aviation industry analyses to senior decision makers up to the Secretary of Transportation. DOT has also incorporated a Modular Procurement Framework for AI and data integration, and is open to AI-assisted analytics, predictive modeling, and enhanced visualization capabilities beyond the minimum SOW requirements, with offerors required to provide a live demonstration of their solution as a decisional criterion for award selection.
periodOfPerformance: Base year + 4 one-year option years (May 1, 2026 through April 30, 2031)
office: Office of the Senior Procurement Executive
title: Airline Analysis and Scheduling Solution via a Web-based Application
placeOfPerformance: DOT Headquarters, 1200 New Jersey Avenue S.E., Washington, DC
